facts: Pluto's surface temperature can drop to -387°F, colder than -233°C
It would take 3,666 football fields lined up to equal Pluto's diameter of 1,475 miles
A day on Pluto lasts 153.3 hours, or about 6.4 Earth days
idea.payoff: and we still don't know why it has a heart-shaped region
idea.title: Pluto: Survival on Ice? 🌌
idea.description: Imagine waking up on Pluto, where the sun rises every 6.4 Earth days 🌅. This mysterious dwarf planet has a surface as cold as -387°F, and a diameter that's equivalent to 3,666 football fields lined up 🏈. Can you survive the harsh conditions on Pluto? 🤔 Watch to find out, and uncover the secrets of its heart-shaped region ❤️

scenes.visual: A stylized, dark, icy Pluto slowly rotating into view, filling the frame. Stars in the background.
Extreme close-up on Pluto's frozen surface, showing ice crystals and a subtle thermometer icon appearing, indicating extreme cold.
Pluto appearing on one side, with a visual representation of Earth or a long line of football fields for scale comparison sliding in from the opposite side.
Pluto rotating very slowly, with a clock icon or a time elapsed graphic appearing next to it, indicating the long day duration.

scenes.text: And why does it have a HEART?
scenes.visual: Pluto rotating to prominently display its iconic 'heart' (Tombaugh Regio), which subtly pulses with a faint glow against the dark space background.
